story: Small towns advertise their secrets if you know where to look. In Landover the secret was children’s things without children. I stayed at a fading motel despite the manager’s warning. I watched an empty school run its route; teachers drank on porches. At Wednesdays, I asked where the kids were. A bald man in mirrored shades leaned from a corner: “See you soon.” He tapped out a message under the table: Room 6. Move his bag. Wait there. Outside, something skittered in an old Fiat, so I floored it back to the motel. My door stood ajar; the room was dark.
implausible_knowledge: He tapped out a message under the table: Room 6. Move his bag. Wait there.
question: Standing before his ajar, dark motel room, what will he do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Get back in the car and flee town without entering the room.
answer_without_knowledge: Step into the dark room to check his things.
